old_claim.note: Heavy Draft Plowing provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No seeder-plow dependency remains because retaining walls and stepped slope fields do not require the distinct Mesopotamian animal-drawn seed-funnel implement.

source_shape.source_locator: The cited water-supply overview concerns water control and infrastructure and provides no seeder-plow requirement for hillside terraces.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Terrace construction and water management are independent of the newly scoped Mesopotamian seed-funnel plow.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old link depended on the obsolete broad phrase heavy draft plowing and disappears when both endpoint scopes are read literally.

ontology_before: A generic plowing label was used to imply that draft tillage enabled retaining-wall terrace construction.
ontology_after: Terrace farming retains its wall and water-management relationships without an unrelated seeding implement.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: terraced_hillside_farming no longer directly depends on heavy_draft_plowing.
would_reject_if: Direct archaeological or agronomic evidence showed the scoped terrace system could only operate with the Early Dynastic Mesopotamian seeder-plow.
short_reason: A Mesopotamian seed-funnel plow is not a prerequisite for building or using terraces.
